g.data-narration: Welcome to Neighborhood Operators in Image Processing. This lesson covers fundamental techniques for processing images using pixel neighborhoods.
Neighborhood operators compute pixel values based on surrounding pixels within a window, unlike point operators that process pixels independently.
g.text: Output depends only on input pixel

g.data-narration: A kernel slides across the image, performing calculations at each position to determine output values for the center pixel.
g.text: How Neighborhood Operations Work: Sliding Window

g.data-narration: Convolution and correlation are the two fundamental operations. Correlation directly multiplies kernel values while convolution rotates the kernel by 180 degrees.

g.text: Directly multiply image and kernel values

g.data-narration: Kernels are odd-dimension matrices (3×3, 5×5) with the center pixel as the reference point for neighborhood operations.
g.text: Kernels are odd-dimension matrices (3×3, 5×5) with center pixel as reference

g.data-narration: Smoothing filters reduce noise by averaging pixel values. The mean filter uses uniform averaging while Gaussian weights pixels by distance from center.
g.text: Reduce noise and detail by averaging pixel values in a neighborhood

g.data-narration: Sharpening filters enhance edges by emphasizing intensity differences. The Laplacian detects rapid intensity changes while unsharp masking subtracts a smoothed version.
g.text: Enhance edges and fine details by emphasizing intensity differences
